steps.title: Find point L
steps.content:
The circle has center $(0,0)$ and radius $3$, so its equation is $x^2+y^2=9$. For $L=(-3,y)$,
$$(-3)^2+y^2=9$$
$$9+y^2=9$$
$$y=0$$

steps.title: Use opposite endpoints
steps.content: Point $J=(3,0)$ and point $L=(-3,0)$ are opposite endpoints of a diameter. The central angle between them is $\pi$ radians.

steps.title: Allow full rotations
steps.content: A positive angle with the same terminal side can be written as an odd multiple of $\pi$. $$\pi+2\pi k=(2k+1)\pi$$ Among the choices, $43\pi$ is an odd multiple of $\pi$. The answer is D.
